findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: Guards all sqlite-fts lexical paths; Turso has no FTS5 virtual tables, so memory_fts will never exist — every FTS5-dependent branch dead-ends.
Throws when engine=sqlite and memory_fts missing; Turso migration must force engine=inmemory or implement a Turso-native FTS replacement.

findings.signature: auto mode: warm in-memory BM25 when !isMemoryFtsAvailable(db)
findings.notes: auto mode falls back to JS BM25 when FTS5 absent; on Turso this path always fires — in-memory index becomes sole lexical engine.

findings.signature: SELECT id, title, content_text, trigger_phrases, file_path FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (?)
findings.notes: Read-only SQL against memory_index; compatible with Turso but better-sqlite3 prepare()/all() API needs wrapper replacement.

findings.signature: SELECT id FROM memory_index ORDER BY id — batched warmup via syncChangedRows
findings.notes: Read-only warmup query; Turso-compatible SQL but better-sqlite3 Statement.all() call needs adapter.

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN learned_triggers TEXT DEFAULT '[]'
findings.notes: DDL migration uses PRAGMA table_info + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN; Turso supports both but PRAGMA output shape may differ — needs adapter validation.

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN learned_triggers
findings.notes: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN requires SQLite 3.35.0+; Turso supports it but PRAGMA table_info shape must be validated in adapter.

findings.signature: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: Reads FTS5 CREATE statement from sqlite_master; Turso has no memory_fts table so verification trivially passes — but callers expecting a definitive check may misbehave.

findings.signature: Column deliberately excluded from FTS5 index
findings.notes: Design invariant (no FTS5 pollution) is migration-irrelevant since Turso lacks FTS5, but column schema must still be created.
Resolves better-sqlite3 Database handle for entity-density scoring; Turso adapter must expose same handle shape or this returns null forever.

findings.signature: getEntityDensityScore(query, db) — reads causal_edges for graph preservation
findings.notes: Entity-density reads causal_edges via db handle; if Turso adapter breaks getDb(), graph preservation silently disabled for all non-complex queries.
Telemetry recording; likely writes to DB. Low migration risk unless telemetry table schema diverges.
Constants consumed by sqlite-fts.ts FTS5 path; irrelevant on Turso (no FTS5), but in-memory BM25 path ignores them — weight semantics change silently.
Engine selection must default away from sqlite on Turso; auto mode needs Turso-aware detection or will try sqlite path and fail.
Orchestrates channel selection; on Turso the 'fts' channel is dead (no FTS5), so every routing tier loses one channel — minimum-channel enforcement may pad unexpectedly.
PRAGMA table_info is better-sqlite3 specific; Turso/libSQL uses different pragma introspection — adapter must normalize column metadata shape.
